The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says SubhanAllah 100 times in the morning and 100 times in the evening, no one will come on the Day of Resurrection with anything better — except one who said the same or more.' (Muslim)
Imam Ali ؓ said: 'The one who knows himself knows his Lord.' Self-knowledge is the beginning of the path.
Rumi said: 'Out beyond ideas of wrongdoing and rightdoing, there is a field. I'll meet you there.' Empty yourself of opinion — that is where Allah is.
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'Adab is everything. Without adab, there is no tariqah.' Carry yourself with courtesy to Allah in every moment.
The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Be in this world as if you were a stranger or a traveller.' (Bukhari). You are passing through — pack light.
Ibn Ata'illah al-Iskandari said: 'How can the heart be illumined while the forms of creatures are reflected in its mirror? Or how can it journey to Allah while shackled by its passions?'
Imam Ghazali said: 'The heart is like a mirror — it reflects whatever faces it. Face Allah and it reflects only Allah.'
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'Don't look at people's faults. Look at your own. The nafs will always give you enough work — you don't need to borrow someone else's problems.'
The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Verily Allah does not look at your appearance or your wealth, but He looks at your hearts and your deeds.' (Muslim)

Rabia al-Adawiyya said: 'O Allah, if I worship You for fear of Hell, burn me in Hell. If I worship You in hope of Paradise, exclude me. But if I worship You for Your own sake, then do not withhold Your Eternal Beauty.'
Ibn Qayyim al-Jawziyya said: 'The tongue is like a lion. If you let it loose, it will wound someone.' Guard your speech — it shapes your reality.
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'The secret of this path is to make your Lord happy. Everything else is secondary.'

The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Wondrous is the affair of the believer — all his affairs are good for him. If something good happens to him, he is grateful and that is good for him. If something bad happens to him, he is patient and that is good for him.' (Muslim)
Imam Shafi'i said: 'I wished that people would learn this knowledge without a single letter being attributed to me.' True knowledge empties you of pride.
Mawlana Jalal ud-Din Rumi said: 'The wound is the place where the Light enters you.' Don't run from your pain — it is an opening.
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'Don't be angry. Anger is from Shaytan. When you are angry, say: A'udhu billahi min ash-Shaytan ir-rajim — until it passes.'
The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The best of you are those with the best character.' (Bukhari). Your akhlaq is your deen in action.
Ibn Ata'illah said: 'Sometimes He opens the door of obedience for you but not the door of acceptance. And sometimes He strikes you with sin, and it becomes a cause of arrival to Him.'
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'Say Bismillah before everything. Even if you forget in the beginning, say it in the middle. Allah accepts.'
Imam Ali ؓ said: 'Three things cannot be retrieved: the arrow once sped from the bow, the word once spoken from the tongue, and the opportunity once gone.'
The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Make things easy and do not make them hard. Give glad tidings and do not make people flee.' (Bukhari). Be a source of mercy, not burden.
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'The greatest jihad is to fight your ego. The nafs is the real enemy — not people, not the world.'
Imam Ghazali said: 'The root of all sin is love of this world.' And the root of all goodness is the love of Allah.
The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Allah is beautiful and He loves beauty.' (Muslim). Beautify your heart — that is what He sees.
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'Be like water — always flowing, always humble, always finding the lowest place. That is where Allah puts His blessings.'
Ibn Qayyim said: 'An hour of reflection is worth more than a year of worship without understanding.' Sit with yourself. Look honestly at your heart.
The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The most beloved of deeds to Allah are the most consistent ones, even if small.' (Bukhari). A small dhikr done daily beats a large dhikr done once.
Mawlana Rumi said: 'I searched for myself and found only God. I searched for God and found only myself.' The two searches are one.
Imam Shafi'i said: 'Whoever desires this world and the Hereafter should seek knowledge, for in it are the treasures of both.'
Mawlana Shaykh Nazim ق said: 'Your heart is a house. Clean it. Sweep out the bad thoughts, the fears, the pride. Then invite Allah in. He will come.'
